title: potentilla rouyana
intro: Potentilla rouyana, also known as the Golden Five-finger, is a perennial herbaceous plant species within the rose family, Rosaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, North Africa, and Asia. Potentilla rouyana has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits including the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
